steps.title: Find the sentence's main verb
steps.content:
The sentence already has a main verb: consist ("consist of one stressed syllable followed by two unstressed syllables"). The blank appears earlier in the sentence, between the subject Trisyllabic words and the examples "such as 'devious' and 'particle'". If the blank introduces a second finite verb, two independent clauses would be joined only by a comma, creating a comma splice.
  • Choice A — classified: past participle — acts as a modifier (words classified as dactyls), not a finite verb; avoids comma splice ✓
  • Choice B — can be classified: finite verb phrase — creates a second independent clause joined to the first by only a comma ✗
  • Choice C — are classified: finite verb phrase — same comma splice error ✗
  • Choice D — have been classified: finite verb phrase — same comma splice error ✗

steps.title: Confirm the correct choice
steps.content:
Only a non-finite participle can fill the blank without competing with the main verb consist. Classified is the only participle; the other choices are all finite verb phrases that produce a comma splice.

That is the correct answer.
